title: Non-electrical triage checklist set (furniture/wood, bikes, ceramics/gluing, jewelry, toys) — 5+ categories
context: Volunteer fixers need consistent triage references across common non-electrical repair categories. Each checklist answers: is this item worth fixing, what are the common faults, what tools are needed, what are the safe-handling notes, and when should a fixer stop. The target categories for this task are: furniture/wood, bikes, ceramics/gluing, jewelry/watches, and toys — at least 5 categories. Each unit must be independently source-cited and license-clean; iFixit guides are link-only (CC-BY-NC-SA, not re-mixable). All are low-to-medium risk (no electrical content). Content license: CC-BY-4.0.
objective: Author at least 5 non-electrical triage checklists (furniture/wood, bikes, ceramics/gluing, jewelry/watches, toys), each conforming to the data-002 schema, source-cited from reuse-permitted sources, reviewed by an approver distinct from the author, and passing all CI content-governance gates.
acceptanceCriteria: At least 5 separate checklist units are delivered: furniture/wood, bikes, ceramics/gluing, jewelry/watches, and toys
Each checklist covers: is-it-worth-fixing assessment, common faults, tools needed, safe-handling notes, and clear when-to-stop criteria
Every factual claim in each checklist is traceable to a cited, reuse-permitted source with retrievedDate, sourceLicense, and reusePermitted recorded; iFixit content is linked, not copied
No third-party logos, trademarks, or the 'Repair Café' name appear in any unit
Each unit is reviewed by an approver distinct from the author; a PR-tied review-log entry is written for each unit recording reviewer, approver, and sources checked
All units render from the data-002 schema, pass the CI content-governance gate, print legibly (WCAG AA contrast, minimum font size, no color-only meaning), and are published under CC-BY-4.0
output: At least 5 committed, schema-valid non-electrical triage checklists (CC-BY-4.0) for furniture/wood, bikes, ceramics/gluing, jewelry/watches, and toys, each with source-cited claims, distinct reviewer/approver, and a PR-tied review-log entry.
